2606.09724.title: Beyond Probabilistic Similarity: Structural, Temporal, and Causal Limitations of Retrieval-Augmented Generation in the Legal Domain
2606.09724.paper_summary: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) has become a standard architectural response to unreliability in legal AI, yet high-profile failures, including fabricated citations submitted to courts and anachronistic legal content presented as current, continue to appear across jurisdictions. We argue that these failures are not residual confabulations to be eliminated by scaling language models, but symptoms of an architectural mismatch between probabilistic retrieval and the hierarchical, temporal, and institutional structure of legal knowledge. We develop the argument in three moves. First, we articulate the ontological commitment of legal knowledge as a triad of properties derivable from classical legal theory: hierarchical and mereological structure, diachronic dynamism under operational closure, and causal traceability of institutional provenance grounded in the duty of justification. Second, we identify three corresponding pathologies of retrieval (mereological blindness, diachronic blindness, and causal opacity), each developed with an operational definition, a failure mechanism, a canonical example, and detection criteria for diagnostic use. Third, we review the state of the art through this lens, showing that existing approaches address these requirements unevenly and do not yet compose into a paradigm that treats them as co-constitutive. From this analysis we derive four architectural commitments that characterize the deterministic-by-design direction for legal retrieval: ontological primacy, event reification, bitemporal correctness, and deterministic interaction protocols. The framework concerns quaestio juris (which norms apply and in what state) rather than the downstream tasks that act on identified norms, and addresses legislative and constitutional retrieval primarily, with interpretive time as an explicit extension.
2606.09724.paper_summary_zh: 檢索增強生成（RAG）已成為法律人工智慧中對不可靠性的標準架構回應，然而，包括向法院提交的虛構引用和作為當前呈現的過時法律內容等高調失敗，仍在各個司法管轄區中出現。我們認為這些失敗並非可通過擴展語言模型來消除的殘餘虛構，而是概率檢索與法律知識的分層、時間性和制度結構之間的架構不匹配的症狀。我們將論點分為三個部分來發展。首先，我們闡明法律知識的本體承諾，作為從經典法律理論中推導出的三元性質：分層和部分結構、在運作閉合下的歷時動態，以及基於辯護義務的制度來源的因果可追溯性。其次，我們確定了檢索的三種相應病理（部分盲點、歷時盲點和因果不透明），每種病理都具有操作定義、失敗機制、典型例子和診斷使用的檢測標準。第三，我們通過這個視角回顧了當前的技術狀態，顯示現有方法對這些要求的處理不均勻，尚未組成一個將它們視為共同構成的範式。從這一分析中，我們推導出四個架構承諾，這些承諾特徵化了法律檢索的設計決定論方向：本體優先性、事件具象化、雙時間正確性和決定論互動協議。該框架關注法律問題（哪些規範適用以及在何種狀態下）而非對已識別規範進行操作的下游任務，並主要針對立法和憲法檢索，將解釋時間作為明確的延伸。
2606.09724.author: Hudson de Martim et.al.
